title: manihot anomala subsp. pavoniana
intro: Manihot anomala subsp. pavoniana, also known as the wild cassava or tapioca, is a perennial plant species native to Central and South America. The plant is known for its edible tubers and medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The tubers of Manihot anomala subsp. pavoniana contain compounds that have been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, to promote overall health and well-being.
